source.notes: Curated down to a smaller set of high-signal missions aligned to the new Automations and software-factory direction.

main_tracks.name: Always-On Agents
main_tracks.description: Webhook and schedule driven agents that stay useful after the demo.

main_tracks.name: Review + QA
main_tracks.description: Reliability systems, verifiers, incident responders, and evidence-driven debugging.

main_tracks.name: Agent Runtime Tools
main_tracks.description: Skills, MCP tools, model routing, and decision systems that make agents more capable.

main_tracks.name: Software Factory
main_tracks.description: Systems that continuously build, test, coordinate, and improve codebases.

side_challenges.name: Best Cursor-Native Workflow
side_challenges.description: Judges can award up to the full shared bonus pool here if this is the defining strength of the project.

side_challenges.name: Best Developer Tool
side_challenges.description: Judges can award part or all of the shared bonus pool here based on real utility for engineering teams.

side_challenges.name: Best Reliability System
side_challenges.description: Judges can award part or all of the shared bonus pool here when verification and trust are exceptional.

side_challenges.name: Most Technically Ambitious
side_challenges.description: Judges can award up to the full shared bonus pool here for an unusually hard technical reach.

side_challenges.name: Best Demo
side_challenges.description: Judges can award part or all of the shared bonus pool here when the presentation quality is a standout.

side_challenges.name: Best Use of AI Safety
side_challenges.description: Judges can award part or all of the shared bonus pool here for projects that demonstrate responsible and safe AI practices.

side_challenges.name: Best Use of Open Claw
side_challenges.description: Judges can award part or all of the shared bonus pool here for projects that make excellent use of Open Claw.

organizer_learnings: Use Eric tweets as the inspiration layer, not as the final taxonomy.
Cut noisy one-off ideas and bias toward repeatable engineering workflows.
Reward demos that show trigger to agent to verification to artifact, not just a chat transcript.
A smaller track set makes judging clearer and examples more credible.
Side challenges should add energy without becoming substitute tracks.

organizer_tools.name: Cursor Automations
organizer_tools.use: Always-on agents triggered by schedules, PRs, Slack, incidents, or webhooks.

organizer_tools.name: Cloud Agents
organizer_tools.use: Background implementation, testing, and artifact generation.

organizer_tools.name: Bugbot Autofix
organizer_tools.use: Review and repair loop for issues detected in pull requests.

organizer_tools.name: MCP Servers
organizer_tools.use: Connect business systems, observability stacks, and internal tools.

organizer_tools.name: Subagents + Skills
organizer_tools.use: Parallel specialization and reusable agent capabilities.

judging_format.suggested_shortlisting: Shortlist by track winners first, then compare finalists on workflow value and demo clarity.

bounties.tweet_text: today we're launching automations! it allows you to trigger agents based on real world events, like incidents, PR's, slack messages or anything that you can connect with a webhook!

analysis.task_brief: Build a production-style automation that triggers on a real event or schedule, completes useful work, and leaves behind an auditable artifact.
acceptance_criteria: Live trigger from webhook, schedule, Slack, PR, incident, or issue
Agent completes work without manual prompting during the run
Output includes a verifiable artifact such as PR, comment, report, or fix
analysis.notes: Manual curation from Eric's launch tweet for Automations.

bounties.title: Build an automation that wakes up on its own

bounties.tweet_text: this morning i woke up to 5 PRs that i kicked off with cloud agents last night. bugbot had reviewed all of them, suggested fixes, and auto-pushed them while i was sleeping.

analysis.idea_theme: Overnight PR swarm with review and self-correction
analysis.task_brief: Create a workflow that can start work asynchronously, review itself, and return mergeable changes by morning.
acceptance_criteria: Background agent opens or updates at least one code change automatically
A second verification step reviews or tests the change
Demo shows the full unattended loop from kickoff to proposed result
analysis.notes: Strong example of hands-off overnight execution.

bounties.title: Wake up to merge-ready PRs

bounties.tweet_text: Bugbot Autofix is now live in Beta! If you enable this, Bugbot will automatically kick off a Cloud Agent to address the bugs it finds and come back to you with code changes ready to be merged in.

analysis.idea_theme: Close the loop on bug finding and bug fixing
analysis.task_brief: Build a reviewer that does more than comment: it should diagnose issues, propose a fix, and validate the change before handing it back.
acceptance_criteria: System identifies at least one concrete issue automatically
System proposes or applies a fix with evidence
Validation step confirms the fix with tests, checks, or repro data
analysis.notes: Maps directly to Autofix and agentic review loops.

bounties.title: Ship a reviewer that can repair what it finds

bounties.tweet_text: 8. debug mode for the tricky ones instead of guessing, it generates multiple hypotheses, instruments code with logging, asks you to reproduce while collecting data, analyzes actual behavior, and makes targeted fixes based on evidence.

analysis.idea_theme: Evidence-driven debugging instead of prompt guessing
analysis.task_brief: Prototype a debug workflow that forms hypotheses, gathers runtime evidence, and narrows toward a targeted fix.
acceptance_criteria: Collects runtime signals such as logs, traces, screenshots, or metrics
Shows at least two candidate explanations before converging
Produces a fix or root-cause report grounded in observed behavior
analysis.notes: Useful anchor for trust and verification.

bounties.title: Build a debugger that gathers evidence before acting

bounties.tweet_text: Cursor can now use multiple browsers at once with subagents.

analysis.idea_theme: Parallel browser agents for research and QA
analysis.task_brief: Build a multi-browser verifier that checks different states, environments, or user journeys in parallel.
acceptance_criteria: At least two browser workers run distinct checks in parallel
Findings are consolidated into one verdict or action plan
Demo shows a case where parallelism improves coverage or speed
analysis.notes: Strong QA example rooted in Eric's subagent posts.

bounties.title: Run browser QA in parallel with subagents

bounties.tweet_text: We're proposing an open standard for tracing agent conversations to the code they generate. It's interoperable with any coding agent or interface.

analysis.task_brief: Design a trace or provenance system that helps humans audit what an agent changed, why it changed it, and how to replay or review that decision.
acceptance_criteria: Maps generated output back to prompts, tools, or execution steps
Supports human review or debugging of agent behavior
Works across at least two actions, files, or runs
analysis.notes: Trace is best positioned as a trust primitive, not a standalone category.

bounties.title: Give agents an audit trail humans can trust

bounties.tweet_text: Agent Skills are now available in Cursor. Skills let agents discover and run specialized prompts and code.

analysis.idea_theme: Reusable capabilities agents can discover and invoke
analysis.task_brief: Build a capability layer for agents, such as skills, plugins, or reusable workflows that make execution more reliable and composable.
acceptance_criteria: Agent can select or invoke specialized capabilities on its own
Capabilities improve quality, speed, or consistency over a baseline
Demo includes at least one concrete before/after workflow
analysis.notes: Core runtime primitive for the new direction.

bounties.title: Package high-value workflows into discoverable skills

bounties.tweet_text: "install the best store package", checks health scores, security vulnerabilities, weekly downloads, and TypeScript support, then picks and installs the winner. One prompt.

analysis.idea_theme: Agents that make tool choices with evidence
analysis.task_brief: Build a decision engine that researches options, applies selection criteria, and explains why the agent chose one path over another.
acceptance_criteria: Decision pulls from at least two external quality signals
System explains its recommendation in a way a human can review
Chosen action is actually executed or prepared automatically
analysis.notes: Great example of agentic judgment instead of raw automation.

bounties.title: Make agents justify their decisions before they act

bounties.tweet_text: Run your business directly from Cursor, with Midday MCP server.

analysis.idea_theme: Bring real business systems into the agent runtime
analysis.task_brief: Use MCP or similar tool interfaces to let agents work across product, ops, finance, support, or incident systems from one workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Agent reads from or writes to at least one external system
Workflow combines code context with business context
Demo shows a useful cross-tool action, not just a thin API call
analysis.notes: Strong bridge between coding agents and operational systems.

bounties.title: Connect agents to the systems your company actually uses

bounties.tweet_text: We've been working on very long-running coding agents. In a recent week-long run, our system peaked at over 1,000 commits per hour across hundreds of agents.

analysis.idea_theme: Coordinate many agents toward one outcome
analysis.task_brief: Design a system where multiple agents coordinate on one codebase or workflow without collapsing into chaos.
acceptance_criteria: At least two agents coordinate on one shared goal
System shows planning, delegation, or judging behavior
Demo explains how conflicts, duplication, or drift are controlled
analysis.notes: Best anchor for the software-factory track.

bounties.title: Coordinate a team of agents, not just one

bounties.tweet_text: self driving codebases are inevitable, and i was honestly a bit surprised that we ended up here this fast. still a lot of work to have this run in production, but we'll get there!

analysis.idea_theme: Build the factory that improves software continuously
analysis.task_brief: Prototype a self-improving codebase loop that monitors, prioritizes, changes, and verifies software with minimal human intervention.
acceptance_criteria: Loop includes detection, action, and verification
Human oversight point is clearly defined
Demo shows why the system can run repeatedly instead of once
analysis.notes: This is the clearest thesis tweet for the overall hackathon direction.

bounties.title: Build a self-improving codebase loop
